popularStory.headline: Memorial
popularStory.description: Sir Richard de Hoghton 1316-1345, Knight of the Shire in the Parliaments of 1322-27-37. Married Sybilla de Lea, direct descendant of the Lady Godiva, whose lands in Lea still form part of the Hoghton …

zh.lifeSummary: Richard de Houghton是Adam de Houghton IV和Aurelia的兒子，於1278年出生在英國藍卡爾霍利。他於大約1305年與Lady Sybil de Lea在英國英格蘭爾莫利頓結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1341年12月在英國藍卡爾霍格頓享年63歲，埋葬在英國。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Richard de Houghton是Adam de Houghton IV和Aurelia的儿子，于1278年出生在英国藍卡爾霍利。他于大约1305年与Lady Sybil de Lea在英国英格兰爾莫利頓结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1341年12月在英国藍卡爾霍格頓享年63岁，埋葬在英国。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Houghton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from any of numerous places called Houghton or Hoghton. Most of the placenames derive from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, though some have different etymologies; for example, Little Houghton in Eccles (Lancashire), which derives from Old English halh ‘nook, corner of land’ + tūn, and Houghton in Bigbury (Devon), which may derive from an Old English personal name Huhha + Old English tūn.

variants.name: Haughton
meanings: English: habitational name from Haughton (Cheshire, Lancashire, Northumberland, Shropshire, Staffordshire), or Haughton le Skerne (Durham). The placenames derive from Old English halh ‘nook, corner of land’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Houghton .
Irish: adopted for Ó hEacháin ‘descendant of Eachán’, a pet form of the personal name Eachaidh (see Hoey ).
Irish: adopted for Ó hEachtair ‘descendant of Eachtair’, a personal name of uncertain origin. Compare Hoctor .

variants.name: Hufton
meanings: English: habitational name; probably from Stony Houghton in Pleasley (Derbyshire), the name of which derives from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Houghton .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: Richard de Houghton was born in 1278, in Chorley, Lancashire, England as the son of Adam de Houghton IV and Aurelia. He married Lady Sybil de Lea about 1305, in Mollington, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. He died in December 1341, in Hoghton, Lancashire, England, at the age of 63, and was buried in England.

fr.lifeSummary: Richard de Houghton est né en 1278, à Chorley, Lancashire, Angleterre, fils de Adam de Houghton IV et Aurelia. Il a épousé Lady Sybil de Lea environ 1305, à Mollington, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé en décembre 1341, à Hoghton, Lancashire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterré à Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Richard de Houghton wurde 1278 in Chorley, Lancashire, England, als Sohn von Adam de Houghton IV und Aurelia geboren. Er hat um 1305 in Mollington, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom Lady Sybil de Lea geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb im Dezember 1341 im Alter von 63 Jahren in Hoghton, Lancashire, England und wurde in England begraben.

Lui sposato Lady Sybil de Lea circa 1305, nel Mollington, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 4 figlie. ja.lifeSummary: Richard de Houghtonは1278年にイギリスランカシュアイアチオルリーでAdam de Houghton IVとAureliaの息子として生まれた。約1305年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアモリントンでLady Sybil de Leaと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1341年12月にイギリスランカシュアイアホートンで63歳で死去し，イギリスにて埋葬された。

ko.Houghton by Warrington, Lancashire, Engl: 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 허그h턴 비 워링턴
ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Houghton(은)는 1278년 영국 랭카실르 초를리에서 Adam de Houghton IV(와)과 Aurelia의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1305년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 모링턴에서 Lady Sybil de Lea(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1341년 12월 영국 랭카실르 호그h턴에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Richard de Houghton nasceu em 1278, em Chorley, Lancashire, Inglaterra filho de Adam de Houghton IV e Aurelia. Ele casou-se com Lady Sybil de Lea aproximadamente 1305, em Mollington, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em dezembro de 1341, em Hoghton, Lancashire, Inglaterra, com 63 anos, e foi sepultado em Inglaterra.

ru.Houghton by Warrington, Lancashire, Engl: хоугтон би варрингтон, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Richard de Houghton родился в 1278 г. в местности чаурли, ланкашир, Англия. Его родителей звали Adam de Houghton IV и Aurelia. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Sybil de Lea около 1305 г. в местности моллингтон, чешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери. Он умер декабрь 1341 г. в местности хогтон, ланкашир, Англия в возрасте 63 лет и был похоронен в местности Англия.
